It also co-ordinates work of the State Commissioner.<br/>iv. National Trust for the Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities<br/>It was set up under the “National Trust for the Welfare of Persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities” Act, 1999, to empower persons with disabilities and facilitate their acceptance in the general community. <br/>v. Ali Yavar Jung National Institute for the Hearing Handicapped<br/>(AYJNIHH)<br/>Established in August, 1983 for researching all aspects of rehabilitation and education of the hearing handicapped as well as for manufacturing prototypes and distribution of aids and appliances for the hearing handicapped<br/>vi. Pt. Deendayal Upadhyaya Institute for the Physically Handicapped (PDUIPH)<br/>The institute was set up in 1976 to cater for comprehensive rehabilitation services for children and persons with disabilities.<br/>vii. National Institute of Mentally Handicapped (NIMH)<br/>Created in 1984 to empower mentally challenged persons to access means of rehabilitation intervention viz. educational, therapeutic, vocational etc. <br/>viii. National Institute of Visually Handicapped (NIVH)<br/>Founded in 1979, the Institute promotes rights and dignity of persons with visual impairments and provides trained manpower for providing quality education, vocational training and rehabilitation services for the visually impaired. <br/>ix. National Institute for Empowerment of Persons with Multiple<br/>Disabilities (NIEPMD)<br/>Founded in 2005 for providing need based comprehensive rehabilitation and facilitating inclusion of such disadvantaged persons. <br/>x. National Institute for the Orthopaedically Handicapped (NIOH)<br/>Created in 1978 to develop manpower for providing services to the orthopaedically handicapped.<br/>xi. Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation, Training & Research (SVNIRTAR)<br/>Founded in 1984 for developing manpower for rehabilitation of disabled persons and development of models of service delivery programmes in the field of locomotors disability. <br/>xii. National Institute of Social Defence (NISD)<br/>This is a nodal institute founded in 1961, for training and research in the field of social defence. <br/>xiii. Rehabilitation Council of India<br/>The Council was established in 1986 under an Act to regulate and monitor services given to persons with disabilities, to standardize syllabi and maintain a central rehabilitation register of professionals in the field of rehabilitation. <br/>(b) All the above mentioned organisations have specified objectives and manda-te with regard to their target groups and they are functioning in the best interests and satisfaction of their respective target groups. <br/><br/>(c) The role and functions of the Commissions and other organizations are such that they cannot be assigned specific overall targets. However, the number of beneficiaries of projects and programmes implemented by the National Trust and National Institutes, with their financial outlay during the last three years is tabulated below: <br/><br/>(Rs. in crore)<br/>National<br/>Institutes 2011-12 2012-13 2013-14<br/> Beneficiaries Financial<br/>Outlay<br/>(Plan) Beneficiaries Financial<br/>Outlay<br/>(Plan) Beneficiaries Financial<br/>Outlay<br/>(Plan)<br/>National Trust 101986 8.08 64178 5.92 74944 7.11<br/>NISD 3538 9.69 4770 8.29 4828 8.37<br/>NIVH 208842 8.16 212412 15.00 216375 19.80<br/>NIMH 165213 7.54 162066 3.91 163272 12.60<br/>AYJNIHH 88673 6.95 96102 10.30 96980 13.30<br/>NIOH 43868 7.73 45988 4.08 48425 10.20<br/>SVNIRTR 174307 9.44 190035 12.60 220939 16.48<br/>NIEPMD 39117 8.54 65065 7.69 79241 9.26<br/>PDUIPH 15211 6.26 20934 2.30 - 6.28<br/> <br/>(d) The Government has no plan to merge these bodies/commissions.<br/>

title: Amendment in the Disability Act
question_text: <br/>(a) whether Government is contemplating to amend the existing Disability Act, if so, the details thereof,and if not, the reasons therefor;<br/><br/>(b) whether Government had held any discussions with the NGOs, State Governments etc., if so, the details thereof;and<br/><br/>(c) the likely time by which amendments would be made in the Act, for the sake of visually-impaired and physically challenged?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI THAAWAR CHAND GEHLOT)<br/><br/><br/>(a) to (c) A statement is laid on the table of the House. <br/>

title: Funds pending for Chhattisgarh
question_text: (a) whether the funds pending for the State of Chhattisgarh is meant for the Deen Dayal Nihshakt Jan Punarwas Yojana, ADIP Scheme, the Integrated Programme for the elderly and the Alcoholism and Drug Abuse Prevention Scheme;<br/><br/>(b) whether the funds pending under above programmes /schemes would be released immediately in the budget for the year 2014-15; and<br/>(c ) by when sanction would be granted for setting up of a composite Regional Centre Braille Press in Raipur and for organising a division-level employment fair at Jagdalpur, for which sanction letter has already been issued?<br/>
answer_text: MINSTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/>(SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/>(a) & (b): Funds are released under Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS), Scheme of Assistance to Disabled Persons for purchase/fitting of Aids/appliances (ADIP scheme), Integrated Programme for Older Persons (IPOP) and the Scheme of Assistance for Prevention of Alcoholism and Substance (Drugs) Abuse as per norms of the Scheme and subject to completeness of proposals / documents received. However in case of deficiency in documents, the grant is released only after rectification of the deficiency.<br/>(c) State Government of Chhattisgarh has agreed in principle only to set up a Composite Regional Centre in Chhattisgarh. However, other details like city and location are to be finalised by State Government. There is no sanction for organising a division-level employment fair at Jagdalpur.<br/>*******<br/>

title: Research and development activities for differently abled people
question_text: (a) Whether Government has undertaken any Research and Development ( R & D) works for developing new technology for the benefits of the differently –abled people;<br/><br/>(b) if so, details of ongoing R & D activities along with the budget allocated;<br/><br/>(c) whether Government has set up/proposes to set up National Centres/Adarsh Vidyalayas/Special Schools for blind, deaf and dumb persons in the country; and<br/><br/>(d) if so, the details thereof, location and State/UT-wise for the past three years?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/>(a) & (b) According to the information received from Department of Science and Technology, the Department of Science and Technology is implementing a programme “Technology Interventions for Disabled and Elderly (TIDE)” with a mandate to promote basic research, development and adaptation of technology for improving quality of life for benefit of elderly population and disabled people in the country through application of science and technology. <br/><br/> Its ongoing research activities include Research, Design and Development of cost effective and affordable prototypes of assistive devices required by differently abled persons in different built environment; development of protocols; preventive technology packages for persons having disabilities. Some of the technologies developed through research projects under this initiative include:-<br/><br/>• Low-Cost Electronics Refreshable Braille Display for the Visually Impaired <br/>• Development of an assistive device for public bus access for the visually impaired<br/>• Mobile Phone assisted Remote Speech Therapy Platform <br/>• Low Cost Hi-fidelity Audio Signal Enhancement Device for Hearing Disability<br/><br/>• Interactive Teaching Aids and Computer Assisted Interventions for Children with Autism<br/>• Locator and Communicative device for Dementia Patients with GSM and GPS technologies <br/>• Stair Climbing Wheel chair (manual) for up/down Climbing<br/>• Multimodal interface to desktop for the spastic and physically-challenged users<br/>• Coherent Communication System (CCS) for comatose and people with multiple disabilities<br/><br/>An amount of Rs. 300 Lakh has been allocated for the programme.<br/><br/>(c) & (d) National Institute for the Visually Handicapped, Dehradun is running a Special School for the visually impaired at Dehradun. There is a proposal to establish college for students with hearing impairment in each of the five zones of the country. A Central Sector Scheme for this purpose has been finalized recently. As per the Scheme, funds are envisaged to be provided to existing colleges to upgrade their facilities. The Screening Committee set up under the scheme has the mandate to identify such colleges in the country.<br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Encouragement for enrolment of disabled children
question_text: <br/>(a) the measures taken by Government to encourage the enrolment of children with disabilities in Government schools; and<br/><br/>(b) whether Government-run schools are required to undertake any special measures or steps to prevent and punish discrimination against children with disabilities to ensure their integration into the mainstream educational institutions, if so, the details thereof, and if not, the reasons therefor?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/>(a) As per information provided by Ministry of Human Resource and Development, Department of School Education and Literacy, under the Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA) programme, the States & UTs are supported to identify, assess, enroll Children with Special Needs (CWSN) in schools and provide them with appropriate assistive devices and supportive teaching learning materials and resource support. Efforts are also made under the SSA to persuade parents and communities to bring such children to schools, through appropriate training and school-parent interaction.<br/> <br/> Their scheme titled “Inclusive Education for Disabled at Secondary Stage (IEDSS)” enables all students with disabilities after completing eight years of elementary schooling to pursue further four years of secondary schooling (Class IX-XII) in an inclusive and enabling environment.<br/><br/>(b) Yes, Sir. Under the Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education (RTE) Act, 2009, States and UT’s have set up decentralized grievance redressal mechanisms, including the State Commission for Protection of Child Right (SCPCR)/Right to Education Protection Authority (REPA), to address complaints.<br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Welfare scheme for differently abled people
question_text: <br/>(a) Whether the Government is implementing welfare schemes for the differently abled people in the country; If so, the list of schemes and the annual target and achievements; <br/><br/>(b) The measures taken by Government to provide adequate opportunities for the differently abled persons in the field of education and employment; <br/><br/>(c) The steps taken by Government to provide reservation for differently abled persons in education and employment; and<br/><br/>(d) The population and various indices of differently abled persons in India and world?<br/>
answer_text: MINSTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE & EMPOWERMENT<br/> (SHRI KRISHNAPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/>(a): Yes, Sir.<br/>A statement containing the list of welfare schemes for the differently abled people in the country along with the annual target and achievements for the year 2013-14 is enclosed at Annexure.<br/><br/>(b) & (c): As per Section 39 of the Persons with Disabilities (Equal Opportunities, Protection of Rights and Full Participation) Act, 1995, all Government educational institutions and other educational institutions receiving aid from the Government, shall reserve not less than three per cent seats for persons with disabilities.<br/><br/>As per Section 33 of of the said Act, every appropriate Government shall appoint in every establishment such percentage of vacancies not less than 3% for persons or class of persons with disability of which one percent each shall be reserved for persons suffering from (i) blindness or low vision; (ii) hearing impairment; (iii) locomotor disability or cerebral palsy, in the posts identified for each disability.<br/><br/>In addition, Under the scheme of Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS) opportunities for the differently abled persons in the field of education and employment are provided by way of providing financial assistance in the form of Grant-in-Aid to NGOs. The major components of the scheme are:-<br/>(i) Vocational Training Centres,<br/>(ii) Sheltered Workshop<br/>(iii) Special Schools<br/>(iv) Project for Pre-School and Early Intervention and Training.<br/><br/>(d): As per Census 2011 of India, there are 2.68 crore persons with disabilities who constitute 2.21 per cent of the total population, out of which about 1.50 crore are males and 1.18 crore are females. As per Census 2001, 49 per cent of persons with disabilities were literate, out of which graduate and above constituted 3 per cent. Moreover, only 34 per cent of persons with disabilities were employed as per the 12 th Plan Working Group Report.<br/><br/>According to estimate of WHO Global Burden of Disease study of 2004, 15.3 per cent of the world population (some 978 million people of the estimated 6.4 billion) had “moderate or severe disability”, while 2.9 per cent or about 185 million experienced “severe disability”.<br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Disability Rehabilitation Centre in every district
question_text: (a) the total number of District Disability Rehabilitation Centres functioning in the country at present, State-wise along with locations thereof;<br/>(b) whether each district in the country has a Disability Rehabilitation Centre, if so, the details thereof;<br/>(c) If not, the steps taken by Government to set up one such centre in each district of the country; and <br/>(d) the total amount sanctioned and spent for the District Disability Rehabilitation Centre (DDRC) during the last three years, State-wise? <br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/>(SHRI THAAWAR CHAND GEHLOT)<br/><br/><br/>(a) to (d) : A Statement is laid on the Table of the House. <br/><br/><br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Enrolment of children with disability
question_text: <br/>(a) the number of children with disabilities enrolled in Government run schools and the State-wise breakup thereof; and<br/><br/>(b) the percentage of children with disabilities as against the total number of children enrolled in Government schools and the State-wise breakup thereof?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/><br/>(a) & (b) According to the Ministry of Human Resource Development, Department of School Education and Literacy, as per the Unified-District Information System for Education (U-DISE) 2013-14, 25.03 lakh Children with Special Needs (CWSN) are enrolled in schools. Besides, 2.06 lakh severe/profound CWSN are covered under Home Based Education (HBE). These together account for 1.36% of total enrolment of all children. The State-wise details on enrolment of CWSN are at Annexure. Under the Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA) programme, the States & UTs are supported to identify, assess, enroll CWSN in schools and provide them with appropriate assistive devices and supportive teaching learning materials etc. Efforts are also made under the SSA to persuade parents and communities to bring such children to schools, through appropriate training and school-parent interaction.<br/><br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Schemes for mentally ill children
question_text: <br/><br/>(a) whether Government has made any plan to take care of the children inflicted with mental illness; if so, State-wise details thereof; and<br/><br/>(b) the number of children inflicted with disease detected during the last three years along with details thereof, State-wise?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/><br/>(a) As per Census 2011, the number of persons with mental illness State-wise is at annexure. Under Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS), the Central Government is providing grant-in-aid to Non-Governmental Organisations for project relating to rehabilitation of persons with disabilities including children with mental illness to enable them to reach and maintain their optimal, physical, sensory, intellectual, psychiatric, or social functional levels.<br/><br/>(b) The Ministry has not conducted any separate survey regarding number of children with mental illness and as such year-wise data in this regard is not available. <br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Schemes and funds for mentally and physically disabled
question_text: <br/>(a) whether persons with mental/physical disabilities including children are reportedly living in inhuman conditions, the details thereof;<br/><br/>(b) whether Government has implemented any schemes/programmes for the welfare of persons with mental/physical disabilities in the country; and <br/><br/>(c) if so, the details thereof including the funds allocated for the purpose during each of the last three years and the current year?<br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE FOR SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT<br/><br/> (SHRI KRISHANPAL GURJAR)<br/><br/><br/>(a) The Ministry has not conducted any survey in this regard.<br/><br/>(b)&(c) Welfare of persons with disabilities is primarily a State subject. However, the Central Government’s certain schemes supplement the efforts of the States in this regard. Major schemes of the Ministry for the purpose of welfare of persons with disabilities are:<br/>(i) Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS) for providing financial assistance through Non-Governmental Organizations (NGOs) to run special schools, vocational training programmes, early intervention and rehabilitation programmes etc.,<br/><br/>(ii) Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship (RGNF) scheme for providing fellowship for pursuing M.Phil and Ph.D courses. <br/>...2/-<br/><br/>-2-<br/><br/>(iii) Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids/Appliances (ADIP Scheme) under which financial assistance is provided to various implementing agencies such as NGOs, National Institute, District Rehabilitation Centres etc. to provide aids and appliances to the needy persons with disabilities<br/><br/>(iv) Further, Ministry has recently launched other scholarship schemes for students for disabilities namely National Overseas Scholarship Schemes, Pre-matric Scholarship and Post-matric Schemes during the current financial year. <br/><br/>Funds allocated under DDRS, ADIP and RGNF Schemes during the last three years and for the year 2014-15 is as under:<br/><br/> Name of the schemes Funds allocated (Rs. In Crores)<br/> 2011-12 2012-13 2013-14 2014-15<br/>DDRS 120 120 124.85 90<br/>ADIP 100 100 110 110<br/>RGNF 11.98 12.00 12.00 17.00<br/><br/><br/>*****<br/>

title: Minority rights to Christian minorities homes
question_text: (a) whether the Christian minorities homes for the destitutes be given the Minority rights for governing the institution and bringing out various welfare measures like establishing Vocational Training Institutions for providing self employment opportunities to empower disabled and rural poor minority people; and<br/><br/>(b) if so, the steps taken by Government for providing easy educational loans for rural minorities?<br/><br/>
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MINORITY AFFAIRS<br/> (SHRI MUKHTAR ABBAS NAQVI)<br/><br/>(a) This Ministry does not grant minority rights to institutions. Further, no such scheme of Vocational Training is implemented by this Ministry. However, a scheme for skill development of minorities “Seekho aur Kamao” is being implemented since 2013-14, which aims at upgrading the skills of the minority youths in various Modular Employable Skills (MES) recognized by Directorate General of Employment & Training (DGE&T), depending upon their educational qualification, present economic trends and the market potential. The scheme is implemented by selected expert Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) which guarantee placement of minimum 75% trained candidates and out of them 50% in the organized sector. <br/><br/>(b): There is no exclusive scheme for providing easy educational loans for rural minorities. National Minorities Development and Finance Corporation (NMDFC), a Central Public Sector Enterprise under Ministry of Minority Affairs, extends educational loans to eligible minorities with an objective to facilitating job oriented education. Under the Scheme, a maximum amount of Rs. 10.00 lakh is provided at the rate of Rs. 2.00 lakh per annum for professional and job oriented Degree courses with a maximum duration of five years. An amount of Rs.3.00 lakh is provided for short term high skill development courses having duration of upto one year. Further, for courses abroad, a maximum amount of Rs. 20.00 lakh is given @ Rs.4.00 lakh per annum for a course duration of maximum 5 years. Funds for this purpose are made available to the beneficiaries at 3% interest per annum. The Scheme is implemented by State Channelising Agencies (SCAs) nominated by respective State Governments.<br/><br/>***<br/>

title: Functioning of MGNREGA
question_text: (a) the number of families registered and the number of job cards issued under the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA) in Uttar Pradesh during the current year;<br/>(b) the details of welfare measures available for workers at work places under MGNREGA;<br/>(c) whether a large number of people living below the poverty line are being deprived of the benefits of MGNREGA;<br/>(d) if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor; and<br/>(e) the corrective measures taken by Government for resolving this issue?<br/>
answer_text: (a): 152.5 lakhs households have been issued job cards so far in UP under MGNREGA. Of this 1.45 lakh households have been registered and 1.00 lakh households have been issued job cards during the current year (till date).<br/><br/>(b): Welfare measures under MGNREGA are elaborated in Paragraphs 25 to 28, Schedule II of MGNREGA 2005. The details of such welfare measures for the workers at work places are:<br/><br/>(i) provision of facilities at the worksite viz. safe drinking water, shade for children and periods of rest, first-aid box with adequate emergency material for treatment of minor injuries and other health hazards connected with the work being performed at the worksite.<br/>(ii) provision of child care services at any worksite if the number of children below the age of five years accompanying the women working is five or more.<br/>(iii) free medical treatment to a person in case of personal injury arising out of and in course of his/ her employment.<br/>(iv) hospitalisation of the injured worker including accommodation, treatment, medicines and payment of daily allowance which should not be less than half of the wage rate.<br/>(v) ex-gratia as per entitlements under Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana or as may be notified by Central Government to a person if he/she while employed under the Scheme meets with death or becomes permanently disabled.<br/>(vi) free medical facility/ ex-gratia to an injured child accompanying a person who is employed under the Scheme.<br/><br/>(c) to (e): No, Madam. As per the provisions of the MGNREGA, any person who volunteers to do unskilled manual work shall be provided work on demand. The Act has neither preference for nor discrimination against any household for the purpose of providing work as per demand. However, the Central Government has taken the following steps to improve the programme implementation under MGNREGA:-<br/><br/><br/>• At least 60% of the works to be taken up in a district in terms of cost shall be for creation of productive assets directly linked to agriculture and allied activities through development of land, water and trees. <br/>• The wage-material ratio for works taken up by agencies other than Gram Panchayats would be counted at the district level to facilitate taking more durable assets. <br/>• The associated outcomes of each work would be estimated before taking up the work and the same would be measured after completion of the work-thereby bringing in more focus on outcomes. <br/>• The quality of the assets is sought to be improved through better planning and closer technical supervision. <br/>• States/UTs have been asked to operationalize the provision relating to compensation for delayed wage payments as per Para-29, Schedule II of MGNREGA 2005.<br/>• States/UTs have been asked to strengthen Social audits of MGNREGS works in accordance with the provisions of the Audit of Schemes Rules 2011 issued in consultation with the Comptroller and Auditor General of India.<br/>• With a view to avoid bogus attendance and to check instances of tempering and misuse of muster rolls, the e-Muster system has been introduced.<br/>• For smooth fund flow, the electronic Fund Management System (e-FMS) has been introduced which would also reduce delays in payment of wages.<br/>• All States have been requested to appoint Ombudsman at the district level for grievance redressal. <br/>• The Ministry has established a comprehensive system of monitoring and review mechanism for MGNREGA, which, inter alia, include visits of Area Officers of the Ministry and National Level Monitors and Vigilance & Monitoring Committee meetings at the State/ District levels.<br/>• To facilitate states to engage technical assistants/barefoot engineers for better technical planning and supervision of works under MGNREGA, guidelines have been issued to allow their establishment cost as a part of material cost of works instead of administrative cost.<br/>• Formulation of State Convergence Plan in 21 States.<br/><br/>******<br/>

title: Implementation of SGSY
question_text: (a) whether the approach paper to the Twelfth Five Year Plan has set a target for reduction of poverty and creation of high quality gainful employment during the Plan period;<br/>(b) if so, to what extent the target has been achieved, so far;<br/>(c) whether the Swarnjayanti Gram Swarojgar Yojana (SGSY) is intended to provide benefits to SCs and STs, disabled and women-headed households who form the bulk of rural poor:<br/>(d) if so, the extent to which SGSY is able to provide benefits to these sections so far; and<br/>(e) the steps taken by Government to accelerate identification of BPL families to give them benefits of gainful employment?<br/>
answer_text: (a) The Twelfth Five Year Plan has the target of reducing head-count ratio of consumption poverty by 10 percentage points and generating 50 million new work opportunities in the non-farm sector over the plan period. <br/><br/>(b) The Government was earlier implementing Swarnjayanti Gram SwarozgarYojana (SGSY), which has since been restructured as National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM). NRLM has been launched in June, 2011. It aims at building strong and sustainable grass roots institutions of rural poor women and enabling them to access their own social networks, resources and knowledge for gainful self-employment and skilled wage employment opportunities and thereby achieve appreciable improvement in their livelihoods on a sustainable basis. The concurrent evaluation study of SGSY programme shows that a large number of SGSY beneficiaries have crossed poverty line across rural areas of the country. <br/><br/>As per the latest estimates of poverty by the Planning Commission, the rural poverty ratio in the country has come down from 41.8% in 2004-05 to 25.7% in 2011-12. <br/><br/>(c) & (d) SGSY has been restructured as National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM) to be implemented in a Mission mode. NRLM ensures special focus on the mobilization of women from the Scheduled Castes (SCs) and the Scheduled Tribes (STs), the Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs), the disabled and other vulnerable and marginalized households. The details of SC and ST Self Help Groups (SHGs) assisted during the current year are given at Annexure.<br/><br/>(e): The target groups under N.R.L.M are now determined by a well defined, transparent and equitable process of Participatory Identification of Poor (PIP) at the level of the community, delinking it from BPL list. The list finalized through the P.I.P process is vetted by the Gram Sabha and approved by the Gram Panchayat. <br/>******<br/>

title: Showcasing Indian culture to foreign tourists
question_text: (a) whether Government has taken any initiative to showcase Indian culture to the foreign tourists visiting the country, if so, the details thereof; <br/><br/>(b) whether Government has undertaken any review to know the utility and impact of the programmes of the Ministry to showcase Indian culture to the foreign tourists; <br/><br/>(c) if so, the details thereof during each of the last three years and current year and if not, the reasons therefor; and <br/><br/>(d) the details of flagship programmes implemented by Government for promoting Indian culture in the country and abroad?
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE, CULTURE AND TOURISM (INDEPENDENT CHARGE) AND MINISTER OF STATE, CIVIL AVIATION<br/><br/>(DR. MAHESH SHARMA)<br/><br/>(a) The Ministry of Culture conducts regular programmes to disseminate Indian Art and Culture in India and abroad. These programmes are accessible to all and not specific to foreign tourists. <br/><br/>(b)& (c) Question does not arise. <br/><br/>(d) There are no such flagship programmes.

title: Preservation and digitization of vast manuscript wealth
question_text: (a) whether Government has any plans to preserve and digitize vast manuscript wealth of the country; and<br/><br/>(b) if so, the details thereof ?
answer_text: MINISTER OF STATE (IC) FOR CULTURE & TOURISM AND <br/>MINISTER OF STATE FOR CIVIL AVIATION <br/><br/>(DR. MAHESH SHARMA)<br/><br/>(a)&(b) Yes, Sir. The National Mission for Manuscripts (NMM) was established in the year 2003 to document, preserve and digitize the vast manuscript wealth of India. Through its Manuscript Resource Centres (MRCs), the NMM has documented 31,23,000 manuscripts and a total of 185,88,390 pages have been digitized up to the end of 2014. 31 Manuscript Conservation Centres (MCCs) are identified across the country which conserve manuscripts with the help of grants from NMM.<br/><br/>Besides this, the National Archives of India operates two schemes for providing financial assistance for preservation of documentary heritage including manuscripts. These are:<br/><br/>(i) Scheme of Financial Assistance for Preservation and Conservation of Manuscripts, Rare Books, Old and Rare Documents, Record of History to Registered Voluntary Organizations/Individuals etc.<br/><br/>(ii) Scheme of Financial Assistance to State/Union Territory Archival Repositories, Government Libraries and Museums. <br/><br/>***

title: OBC reservation in Central universities
question_text: (a) whether OBC reservation is being implemented/followed in recruitment/filling up of all faculty posts of Professors/Associate Professors/Assistant Professors in Central universities and if so, since when; <br/><br/>(b) the measures taken to fill the backlog of OBC faculty posts in Delhi University;<br/><br/>(c) whether Government have given any direction to Delhi University in this regard and the action taken by DU; and<br/><br/>(d) whether Government has issued any guidelines to colleges of DU with regard to ‘Roster Rules’ in order to fill faculty posts and if so, the details thereof?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>(a) to (d) The UGC had issued a circular to all Central Universities with the request to implement the 27% reservation policy of Govt. of India in respect of OBCs for filling up the Teaching posts at the level of Lecturer/Assistant Professor with immediate effect except Minority Institutions under Article 30 (1) of the Constitution of India.<br/><br/> The UGC has issued various instructions from time to time to all the Universities/Institutions Deemed to be Universities (receiving grant-in-aid from the public fund) for (i) implementation of SC/ST/PWD/OBC Reservation Policy of the Govt./UGC and also for (ii) filling up of backlog reserved vacancies of these categories in teaching and non-teaching posts except in Minorities Institutions under Article 30(1) of the Constitution.<br/> <br/> Further, the UGC has since requested all the Central Universities for (i) strict compliance of reservation policy, (ii) display of reservation roster on University website and (iii) filling up of remaining identified backlog vacancies for SC/ST/OBC as on 1.11.2008 and Persons with Disabilities as on 15.11.2009.<br/><br/> UGC has also informed that while releasing the grant to the Colleges affiliated to University of Delhi, it is mentioned in all sanction letters that college should strictly follow all the instructions issued by Government of India from time to time with regard to reservation of SC/ST/OBC posts.<br/><br/>*****

title: Implementation of reservation rules in Central universities
question_text: (a) whether Government has constituted any regulatory body to look into the proper implementation of reservation rules for filling up the faculty posts in Central universities;<br/><br/>(b) whether it is a fact that there is no transparency in filling up of the various faculty posts in Central universities and the UGC guidelines in this regard;<br/><br/>(c) whether Government follows the social justice norms in appointing Vice-Chancellors/Principals in various universities/colleges; and;<br/><br/>(d) the number of OBCs, SCs and STs who have been appointed as VCs/Principals in Delhi University/Colleges of DU, the details along with the names etc. in this regard?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>(a) to (d) The Government, in exercise of the powers vested under Section 20(1) of the University Grants Commission (UGC) Act, 1956, has directed the UGC to ensure effective implementation of the reservation policy in the Central Universities and those of institutions Deemed to be Universities receiving aid from the public funds except in Minority Institutions under Article 30(1) of the Constitution. <br/> <br/> UGC issues instructions from time to time to all Central Universities for (i) implementation of SC/ST/PWD/OBC Reservation Policy of the Govt./ UGC (ii) strict compliance of reservation policy, (iii) display of reservation roster on University website and (iv) filling up of remaining identified backlog reserved vacancies of these categories in teaching and non- teaching posts. <br/> The Guidelines issued by UGC for strict implementation of reservation policy of the Government in Universities, Deemed to be Universities, Colleges and other Grant-in-Aid Institutions and Centres, is available on the website of UGC at http://ncst.nic.in/writereaddata/linkimages/UGC%20Guidlines_for%20SC_ST6052135002.pdf. <br/> A Statement of Teaching position as on 01.01.2015 (Category-wise) indicating sanctioned/existing/vacant positions in Central Universities including University of Delhi, under the purview of UGC is at Annexure-I. However, information of Colleges of University of Delhi is not maintained centrally. <br/><br/>*****

title: Integrated Education for Disabled Children
question_text: (a) whether the previous Government had stopped/replaced the scheme of Integrated Education for Disabled Children’ (IEDC), if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor; <br/><br/> (b) the likely adverse effects on the education of disabled children as a result thereof; and <br/>(c) whether Government is contemplating to restart the said scheme and if so, the details thereof?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>(a) to (c) The Scheme of “Integrated Education for the Disabled Children (IEDC)” was implemented till 31.3.2009, and thereafter, it has been replaced by the Scheme of “Inclusive Education for the Disabled at Secondary Stage (IEDSS)” which covered IEDC disabled children at both elementary and secondary levels, children with special needs in the elementary stage are now being covered under the Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), and the scheme of IEDSS covers disabled children at the secondary and Higher Secondary stage (class IX-XII). <br/><br/>********

title: Education of children with learning disability
question_text: (a) whether Government has taken any steps to facilitate the education of children with learning disability in the country, if so, the details thereof;<br/><br/>(b) the details regarding the concessions provided by different Central and State Boards of Education for the children with learning disability and the details regarding the number of children benefited from them;<br/><br/>(c) the number of children with learning disability in the country; and<br/><br/>(d) the steps taken by Government to facilitate the early identification and care and specialised training for the children with learning disability?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/><br/>(a) to (d): Yes, Sir. Government of India is implementing the Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), a programme for universalising elementary education for all children from 6-14 years of age including children with learning disabilities. A total of 25.03 lakh Children with Special Needs (CWSN) have been enrolled in elementary education & 2,06,713 CWSN are receiving home based education.<br/><br/>SSA promotes inclusion in schools for children with special needs (CWSN) through their early identification, educational placement, provision of aids and appliances and other resource support services, teacher training, parental training, community mobilisation, curricular adaptations, removal of architectural barriers, as well as residential, non residential or even home based education, as per their specific requirements. The National Council of Educational Research & Training (NCERT) has developed exemplar material on curricular adaptations, inclusive teaching and how to adopt flexibility in evaluation for children with disabilities in inclusive classrooms. This handbook, meant for primary level teachers, covers all disabilities, including cognitive and intellectual disabilities.<br/><br/>Further, 2.11 lakh CWSN are enrolled at secondary level under Inclusive Education for Disabled at Secondary Stage (IEDSS), which provides assistance for inclusive education for children with special needs in Classes IX-XII through direct student assistance which includes inter alia, medical & educational assistance, books & stationary, assistive devices, as well as provision for teachers, teacher training etc. The State Governments and Boards of Education are taking various steps such as extra time during examination, reduction in qualifying criteria and passing marks, help of scribe/writer/reader etc. for the aid of children with special needs (CWSN), including those with learning disabilities.<br/> <br/> <br/> *****

title: Reservation for OBCs in Central Universities
question_text: (a) the details of Assistant Professor positions reserved for OBCs since implementation of Mandal Commission recommendations;<br/><br/>(b) whether it is a fact that all Central Universities particularly University of Hyderabad, have not filled- in the shortfall of teaching positions of OBCs by 27 per cent at the level of Assistant Professor;<br/><br/>(c) if so, the details thereof, University-wise;<br/><br/>(d) whether it is also a fact that University Grants Commission is not monitoring the roster register of each central university; and <br/><br/>(e) if so, the reasons therefor?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>(a) to (c) The University Grants Commission (UGC) had issued necessary instructions to all Central Universities for OBC reservation that the 27% OBC reservation is applicable in all Central Educational Institutions in direct recruitment at the Assistant Professor level only w.e.f. 24.1.2007.<br/><br/> A Statement of Teaching position as on 01.01.2015 (Category-wise) indicating sanctioned/existing/vacant positions in Central Universities under the purview of UGC is at Annexure-I.<br/><br/>(d) to (e) The UGC had issued a circular to all Central Universities with the request to implement the 27% reservation policy of Govt. of India in respect of OBCs for filling up the teaching posts at the level of Lecturer with immediate effect except Minority Institutions under Article 30 (1) of the Constitution of India. <br/><br/> The UGC is issuing instructions from time to time to all the Universities receiving grant-in-aid form the public fund for (i) implementation of SC/ST/PWD/OBC Reservation Policy of the Govt./UGC and also for (ii) filling up of backlog reserved vacancies of these categories in teaching and non-teaching posts except in Minorities Institutions under Article 30(1) of the Constitution. <br/><br/> Further, the UGC has since requested all the Central Universities for (i) strict compliance of reservation policy, (ii) display of reservation roster on University’s website and (iii) filling up of remaining identified backlog vacancies for SC/ST/OBC as on 1.11.2008 and Persons with Disabilities as on 15.11.2009 failing which UGC would be constrained to withhold the grant particularly non-plan (non salary) grant.<br/><br/><br/>*****

title: Children suffering from learning disability
question_text: (a) the details regarding the number of children suffering from learning disability in the country, State-wise;<br/><br/>(b) the steps taken by different State Governments and Boards of Education for the aid of children with learning disabilities;<br/><br/>(c) the number of special educators trained in learning disability in the country;<br/><br/>(d) the number of special schools/ NGOs/other organisations in the country which provide specialized training for children with learning disability; and<br/><br/>(e) the steps Government would take to create awareness about learning disability in the country?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>(a): Under Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), a programme for universalising elementary education for all children from 6-14 years of age, 2.84 lakh children with learning disabilities have been enrolled in schools. State wise details are given at Annexure. <br/> <br/>(b) & (c): Under SSA, 20544 resource persons have been appointed to exclusively address the learning needs of children with disabilities. Further, 5641 general teachers have been given specific orientation on learning disabilities. Rehabilitation Council of India has been set up under the Rehabilitation Council of India Act, 1992 (as amended in 2000) as a statutory body to regulate and monitor the training programmes in the field of disability rehabilitation and special education. <br/> <br/>(d) SSA is implemented through the State/UT Governments who have reported taking assistance of 15211 special schools and 764 NGOs in providing different components of inclusive education.<br/><br/>(e) Government has taken steps to facilitate the education of these children. The National Council of Educational Research & Training (NCERT) has recently developed exemplar material on curricular adaptations, inclusive teaching and how to adopt flexibility in evaluation for children with disabilities in inclusive classrooms. This handbook, meant for primary level teachers, covers all disabilities, including cognitive and intellectual disabilities. <br/> <br/> *****

title: Online courses under SWAYAM
question_text: (a) whether any progress has taken place in the above programme in terms of collaborations and content creation;<br/><br/>(b) if so, the details thereof and the reasons therefor;<br/>(c) the current status of the plan for formation of consortium of premier institutions and universities to offer flipped online courses under SWAYAM;<br/><br/>(d) the detailed timeline, budget and implementation roadmap for the programme; and<br/><br/>(e) whether due steps are being taken to make it accessible to village and semi-urban areas with poor internet connectivity?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/><br/>(a) Sir, presently there is no such plan.<br/><br/>(b) to (d) As far as content creation is concerned, under the National Mission on Education through Information and Communication Technology (NMEICT), more than 810 web and video courses in various disciplines in engineering and science are available on-line through National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL). E-content for 8 undergraduate subjects has also been generated by the Consortium of Educational Communication (CEC) in collaboration with its Media Centers. Over 126 Virtual Labs in 9 Engineering & Science disciplines, comprising more than 770 experiments are currently ready for use and available. 1500 Spoken Tutorials are available on line. Further large number of courses for design have been also been created.<br/><br/>(e) The Ministry has no role regarding internet connectivity in villages and semi-urban areas. <br/><br/>*****

title: Disabled friendly rules in universities and educational institutions
question_text: whether Government would direct the universities and educational institutions to amend their rules in respect of projects, evaluation, examination and marking system in a disabled friendly manner like examination in oral format/provision of services and exemption from mandatory written projects for blind candidates etc.?
answer_text: MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT<br/><br/>(SMT. SMRITI ZUBIN IRANI)<br/><br/>The University Grants Commission (UGC) has informed that it has circulated to all Universities the uniform and comprehensive guidelines of the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MOSJE) for conducting examination, in a disabled friendly manner, for the persons with disabilities. These guidelines were issued by the MOSJE in compliance with the Order dated 23rd November, 2012 of the Chief Commissioner of Persons with Disabilities (CCPD) in case No. 3929/2007 (Shri Gopal Sisodia, Indian Association of the Blind Vs. State Bank of India & Others) and in case No.65/1041/12-13 (Score Foundation Vs. Department of Disability Affairs). A copy of these guidelines are available at http://www.ccdisabilities.nic.in/ content/en/docs/omguide.pdf .<br/><br/>*********

title: ESTABLISHMENT OF DIGITAL LIBRARY .
question_text:
(a) Whether it is a fact that Government have decided to establish a digital library in the country;

(b) if so, the details thereof ;

(c) the objective behind establishment of such library ; and

(d) by when it would be established ?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF INFORMATION & BROADCASTING AND CULTURE

(SHRI S. JAIPAL REDDY )


(a) Such a proposal is being formulated.

(b) & (c): It proposes to make available in digital format cultural contents like books, songs, paintings etc. It will facilitate wider dissemination and better accessibility.
Most institutions within Ministry of Culture have on-going invidividual digitization programmes of their material.

(d) Preliminary work is expected to commence during this year to consolidate these efforts.

answer_text:
MINISTRY OF INFORMATION&BROADCASTING AND CULTURE

(SHRI S. JAIPAL REDDY)

(c) to (e): Letters were received from Shri Bhagwati Singh, Member of Parliament and Smt. Sunita Singh, of Lucknow, regarding appointment of a regular incumbent to the post of Director, Rampur Raza Library. title: SETTING UP OF SCHOOL CUM VILLAGE LIBRARIES IN PUNJAB .
question_text:

(b) the number of libraries proposed to be established during the current financial year 1995-96; and

(c) the funding pattern and the method being adopted for their maintenance?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION AND DEPARTMENT OF CULTURE ) (KUMARI SELJA) :

(a) So far no school-cum-village library has been set up anywhere in the country.

(b) and (c) A scheme of School-cum-village libraries has been formulated and is under the process of finalisation in consultation with the concerned agencies.

title: CERTIFICATE COURSE IN LIBRARY SCIENCE .
question_text:
(a) whether some certificate course in library science has been introduced in some Institution of Delhi under instructions of his Ministry;

(b) if so, the details thereof with number of students admitted, eligibilities prescribed, duration, alongwith final examination prescribed and fees to be charged;

(c) whether some scope of employment has also been provided for successful candidates of the newly introduced course; and

(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (SHRI MUHI RAM SAIKIA):

(a) to (d) Under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Vocationalisation of Secondary Education, all State Governments/UTs (except Lakshadweep) including Delhi have introduced a vocational course of Library Science at the +2 level in selected schools. 10th class pass students are eligible for admission to the two years vocational course. States/UTs are expected to enrol 25 students each in classes XI and XII. The examination is conducted by the respective State Boards of Education/Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE). The NCERT competency based curriculum document enlists some job possibilities for the students completing the course of `Library Science` like Library Assistants in School libraries, in Village Community Centre, Adult Education Centre, Nonformal Education Centre, Panchayat etc. There is, however, no job guarantee associated with these courses.

title: FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE BEING OFFERED TO LIBRARIES .
question_text:
(a) whether it is a fact that Government have recognised that importance of Libraries in Literacy movements; and

(b) if so, the guidelines given to State Governments in this regards and details of the financial assistance being offered, if any?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (SHRI MUHI RAM SAIKIA)

(a) Yes, Sir.

(b) Government of India have recognised the importance of Libraries in literacy movements. Under the Scheme of continuing Education forneo-literates, the establishment of Continuing Education Centres (CECs) is the principal instrumentality to enable the neo-literates and other targetted beneficiaries to retain, improve and apply their basic knowledge and skills. The Continuing Education Centre is envisaged to function as a library and there is provision for purchase of books for the Continuing Education Centre.
The Scheme of Continuing Education is circulated to All State Governments for preparation of continuing Education projects. The proposals for Continuing Education for neo-literates from State Government of Andhra Pradesh; for eight districts, State Government of Kerala, for entire state, State Government of West Bengal for Burdwan and Birbhum district are under consideration of Government of India for release of financial assistance during this financial year.
Ordinarily, one continuing Education Centre (CEC) may be established to serve around 500 neo-literates. 8 to 10 Continuing Education Centres would from a cluster, with one of them being designated as a `Noble CEC`. There is uniform pattern comprising of non-recurring provision of Rs. 10,000/-Continuing Education Centre per year. The financial assistance includes the provision for purchase of books upto the extent jof Rs. 2,550/-out of non-recurring and Rs.2,000/-out of recurring funds per Continuing Education Centre per year. The corresponding provisions for the Noble CEC is Rs.20,000/-each for non-recurring and recurring per Noble Continuing Education Centre. The financial assistance included the provision for purchase of books upto the extent of Rs.7,000/-out of non-recurring budget and Rs.2500/-out of recurring budget per year per Nodal Continuing Education Centre.
A Proposal for development of nation-wide village library movement under the Scheme of Continuing Education for neo-literates during the Ninth Five Year Plan period is under consideration of Government.

title: SCHEMES TO INVOLVE LOCAL BODIES IN CULTURAL PROGRAMMES .
question_text:
(a)whether it is a fact that Government propose to involve local bodies Panchayats and Nagar Palikas-in important cultural programmes of the Department;

(b) if so, the details thereof, schemewise; and

(c) if not, the reasons therefor?
answer_text:
ED THE MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (SHRI S.R. BOMMAI): (a) Yes, Sir.

(b) A scheme has been formulated to establish Rural Libraries at Panchayat level. The scheme is to be implemented through the Raja Rammohun Roy Library Foundation in cooperation with the State Govts/Union Territories on a 75:25 matching basis. The scheme aims to provide assistance to Gram Panchayats including local government authority at Block level if there are no village Panchayats. The Centre`s share of the grant is: for construction of library building an amount of Rs. 1 lakh and for purchase of books, furniture and equipment Rs. 50,000/- to effectively run the library activities, the library will be administered at the Panchayat level by a Committee comprised of BDO/Panchayat Pradhan/Sarpanch/Mukhiya in their ex-official capacity and such other representatives as the State Govt. may nominate. To commemorate the 50th Anniversary of India`s Independence, celebrations are being planned in a manner that will ensure participation by a maximum bumber of people, involving all sections of Society and at all levels such as towns, villages, Tehsils, district headquarters, State Capitals, factories, trading centres, schools, colleges, universities, local bodies and panchayti Raj institutions etc. The plan of action for this celebration is to be decided by State level Committees formed for the celebrations of 50th Anniversary of India`s Independence. In this connection Union Minister of Human Resource Development has addressed a detailed communication on 2.6.97 to all Chief Ministers.

(c) Does not arise.

title: SCHEME FOR ESTABLISHING RURAL LIBRARIES .
question_text:
(a) whether Government had announced a scheme, namely Financial Assistance for Establishing Rural Library;

(b) if so, details of the achievement thereof; and

(c) whether Government have faced any problems in running the scheme; if so, whether Government propose to review the scheme?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI)

(a) Govt. has not approved the scheme namely Financial Assistance for Establishment Rural Library.

(b) and (c) Do not arise.

title: TITLES PUBLISHED IN INDIA DURING .
question_text:
(a) the number of titles published in India during 1997-98, language-wise and State/Union Territory-wise, as received in four Depository Libraries under the Delivery of Books Act, 1954;

(b) if the figures received in the Depository Libraries do not reflect the correct position of the books published what action has been taken to remedy the situation;

(c) whether all the four libraries have adequate space to accommodate and service the books received; and

(d) if not, the steps begin taken by Government to provide proper facilities to the libraries?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI): (a) Under the Delivery of Book Act, 1954 there are 4 Depository Libraries

1. National Library, Calcutta

2. Delhi Public Library, Delhi

3. State Central Library,, Mumbai

4. Connemara Public Library, Chennai The number of titles, these depository libraries received in 1997-98 under Delivery of Books Act, 1954 is given in Statement-1 (See below).
The Statewise position in respect of national Library, Calcutta, State Central Library, Mumbai is given in Statement-11 (see below). Connemara Public Library, Chennai and Delhi Pubic Library, Delhi do not maintain Statewise records.

(b) With a view to make the Act more effective, Government of India (Department of Culture) has taken steps to review the Delivery of Books Act, 1954 through a Committee under the Chairmanship of Joint Secretary, Department of Culture.

(c) and (d) The space available in the National Library, Calcutta, Connemara Public Library, Channai and State Central Library, Mumbai needs improvement. Action has been taken to construct a `Bhasha Bhavan` in National Library.

title: RAJA RAMMOHUN ROY LIBRARY FOUNDATION .
question_text:
(a) The number of new libraries instated so far with the initiative of Raja Ram Mohan Roy Library Foundation, since opening of new libraries is one of the main objectives for which the Foundation was created;

(b) the book purchased and supplied engage-wise by the Foundation to the libraries in towns and rural areas seperately during 1997-98 and

(c) what is the procedure for selection of books in the various Indian languages adopted by the Foundation for supply to the libraries?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT (DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI)

(a) Since library is a State subject, the main objective of Raja Rammohan Roy Library foundation is to support the library movement in the country. Hence instituting new library dies not fall under the preview of its objective.

(b) and (c) The Foundation purchases books in (1) Hindi (2) English (3) translations

(Hindi/English) from other Indian language for distribution to the state central libraries, district libraries and such other public libraries as decided by Raja Rammohan Roy Library Foundation from time to time The books are selected by a Book Selection Committee, set up by the Administrative Committee, in accordance with the guidelines laid down for the Committee . The bookes not ordinarily selected are (1) foreign publications (2) text books costing above Rs.500/-.However, exceptions are made in case of valuable and useful books. Selection is normally restricted to books published within the last 3 years , including the year of selection.
As far as books in regional language are concerned these are purchased by the States under the schemes of matching assistance towards building-up up of adequate stock of books and other reading and visual material. Under the scheme the State Government can purchase books in different subjects in regional languages.

title: ALLOTMENT OF LAND TO DR.AMBEDKAR LIBRARY
question_text:
(a) whether the physical possession of the land measuring about four acres allotted to her Ministry In April 1994 for the project of Dr.Ambedkar National Public Library at New Delhi has not yet been handed over to them;

(b) if so, what are the reasons therefor;

(c) what efforts are being made by her Ministry for obtaining physical possession of the above referred land allotted for this project; and

(d) what steps have been taken for procurement of adequate funds for commencement of this project?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT SMT. MANEKA GANDHI

(a) & (b) ; No Sir. Physical possession of land measuring about 4 acres allotted to the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment has not yet been made over as the said land consisting of five bungalows (excluding one bungalow which has been handed over) have been allotted to the members of Parliamen.

(c): The matter is being pursued with the concerned authorities of Ministry of Urban Affairs and Employment and Chairman of the House Committee Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

(d) : An amount of Rs.5.50 crore has been released as Grant for Dr.Ambedkar National Public Library in the year 1991-92. The amount is kept in Fixed Deposit.

(SMT. MANEKA GANDHI)

9. Review of the Project of Dr. Ambedkar Memorial at 26, Alipur Road. 10.Creation of additional posts on regular basis.

title: MATERIAL FOR CHILDREN IN THE WORLD BOOK FAIR .
question_text:
:

(a) what fresh titles for children were produced by the NBT at the occasion of World Book Fair, held by the National Book Trust, India recently, the accent being on reading materials for children;

(b) what specific steps were taken by NBT to promote book mindedness among the children; and

(c) whether it is a fact that the foreign publishers of children’s books overwhelmed the visitors with their publications for children in English, as stated by BBC ? A N S W E R THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT ( SHRI JAYSINGRAO GAIKWAD PATIL )
answer_text:
(a) Keeping in view, the accent on reading material on children, the National Book Trust brought out around 250 titles exclusively for children in various languages during the 14th World Book Fair. These included 14 fresh titles and 51 new translations besides reprints of many important and popular titles.

(b) At the 14th World Book Fair, NBT set up a theme pavilion which included
(i) World of Children’s Books Exhibitions from all over the country and world,
(ii) a one-stop shop for Indian children’s books in Hindi and English and
(iii) an activity centre for book-related children’s activities such as workshops, competitions, story-telling sessions, creative writing workshops, etc. There was a library corner set up for children to browse through books and also for adults to become members of a scheme to start neighbourhood libraries. To promote book mindedness among children, NBT also organised a seminar which explored vital areas of the field of the children’s literature including promotion of book mindedness and setting up of rural libraries.

(c) Neither the Ministry nor the National Book Trust, India is aware of BBC’s report. However, Foreign publishers of children’s books do usually attract attention of the public initially due to their strong emphasis on production quality with several add-ons which make their products more saleable. However, it is also a fact that there was an overwhelming demand for Indian books in Hindi and English as large volume of these were sold during the fair.
OoO

title: ACTIVITIES OF NIVH .
question_text:
(a whether Government are carefully monitoring the activities of the National Institute for the Visually Handicapped (NIVH), based in Dehradun;

(b) what are its present activities;

(c) whether there is any scope to expand its activities to States where no such institute is available;

(d) whether the NIVH will consider proposals to conduct camps outside Dehradun;

(e) if not, the reasons therefor; and

(f) what steps are proposed to bring visually handicapped people into the mainstream?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT (SMT. MANEKA GANDHI)

(a) Yes, Sir.

(b) The present activities of the Institute mainly are :-

(i) Manpower Development :- The Institute conducts long term training programme such as Primary and Secondary level Teacher Training Programme and Orientation and Mobility Instructors Training Programme as well as short term training programme such as Refresher/Orientation Courses and workshops for the professionals and others working in the field of visually handicapped.

(ii) Education and vocational Training : The Institute runs Model School which provides education from pre-school to class 12th and Training Centre for the Adult Blind which provides vocational training in Light Engineering, Chair Caning, Braille Shorthand, Typewriting, Handloom Weaving, Electronics, Orientation and Mobility, Candle and Chalk Making and Telephone Operation.

(iii) Service Programmes : The Institute provides Placement Services, Therapeutic help to newly blinded, Guidance and Counseling and runs National Library for the Print Handicapped and national Talking Books Library.

(iv) Production Activities : The Institute is engage in Manufacture of Braille and runs Central braille Press.

(v) Extension Services : The Institute has established a Regional Centre in Chennai and Regional Chapters at Secunderabad and Calcutta which are engaged in activities like Vocational Training, Placement, Community Based Rehabilitation Short-term Training Programme and library Services.

(vi) Research : The Institute is also engaged in research activities in the field of Psycho-social and educational aspects of visual disability, Development of contradictions and abbreviations in different languages, Development of Braille Shorthand system in different Indian languages and Developments of Prototypes for Assistive Devices.

(c) Yes, Sir.

(d) Yes, Sir. NIVH is already conducting camps outside Dehradun.

(e) Does not arise.

(f) The activities of the Institute in the fields of Education, Vocational Training, provision of Assistive Devices, Employment, Counselling and Guidance, Library Services and Braille/Talking Books will help the visually handicapped to be self-reliant and in bringing them in the mainstream.

title: HIGH COST OF HIGHER EDUCATION .
question_text:
(a) whether it is a fact that the means of higher education are becoming costlier in the country day by day for students and the general public;

(b) whether it is also a fact that Central Government have been adding fuel to fire by increasing fees of Central Universities and membership-fee of public libraries like Sahitya Academy;

(c) if so, whether Government would withdraw such moves;

(a) if so, by when; and

(b) if not, the reasons therefor?
answer_text:
THE MINSITER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT

(DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI)


(a) to (e): The information will be laid on the Table of the House in due course.

title: STATUS OF DR. AMBEDKAR NATIONAL PUBLIC LIBRARY PROJECT
question_text:
(a) whether it is a fact that Government have released an amount of Rs. 5.5 crore during 1992-93 to the project of Dr. Ambedkar National Public Library on Janpath, New Delhi, besides allotting about four acres of land on Janpath, New Delhi for this Project during April, 1994;

(b) if so, the action taken to take physical possession of the land allotted and utilization of funds released for this Project;

(c) if not, the reasons for the same; and

(d) the present status of this Library Project?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT (SMT. MANEKA GANDHI)


(a,b,c&d) The Government of India released an amount of Rs. 5.50 crores for setting up of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar National Public Library. Ministry of Urban Development allotted envelopes no. 6 & 7, which included Bungalow No. 13, 15, 17, 19 and 21 Janpath, New Delhi on Raisina Road institutional area to the Ministry of Welfare (now Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment). Out of 5 bungalows allotted, physical possession of the bungalow no. 21 Janpath was taken over in Oct. 1997 and 15 Janpath in May 2000. Efforts are being made to take physical possession of the remaining bungalows and start the Library Project.

title: HIGH LEVEL COMMITTEE FOR IT SECTOR .
question_text:
(a) whether it is a fact that a high level Committee was appointed by Government to look into the entire range of issues relating to provision of IT manpower for the growing IT sector;

(b) whether the Committee has since given its report;

(c) if so, what are its findings; and

(d) if not by when its findings are likely to be received?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT

(SHRI SYED SHAHNAWAZ HUSSAIN)


(a): Yes sir.

(b)&(c): Details are annexed.

(d): Question does not arise.

ANNEXURE


ANNEXURE TO RAJYA SABHA UNSTARRED QUESTION NO.3708 FOR 22.12.2000 BY SHRI S. RAMACHANDRAN PILLAI REFERRED TO IN PARTS

(b) & (c) OF THE REPLY.

The interim Report of the Task Force on HRD in IT has been finalised and is under print. It will be submitted very shortly. The Task Force has made forty-seven (47) specific recommendations to respond to the IT manpower challenge. The objective is to provide quality IT professionals for both meeting the domestic and global demands. This shall be done by strengthening and upgrading the technical education system in the country. It is proposed to give special focus on increase in intake in ‘Quality’ Institutions and upgrade some of them to improve the quality of their programmes. These recommendations specifically relate to IT faculty development, IT curriculum & courseware development, strengthening of computing and networking facilities, digitization and modernization of libraries, use of IT for administrative support services and promoting interface with industry. Strategy for overall institutional development with networking has been proposed. Measures identified for development of IT faculty include scaling up of Early Faculty Induction Programme(EFIP), Quality Improvement Programme(QIP) and introduction of Sequential Post Graduate Programmes, allowing transmigration and switchover of faculty from interfacing disciplines. It is expected that recommendations, when implemented would result in an overall increase in availability of quality IT manpower in the country. This will help the Indian software services sector to increase its productivity and move up the value chain, enabling the country to capture larger share of global markets of IT software and services.

title: BOOKS PUBLISHED IN INDIAN LANGUAGES
question_text:
(a) What is the latest number of books published in the Indian languages, including English during 1998-99, language- wise and State-wise;

(b) Whether figures supplied by the National Library reflect correct position of the publishing activity in the country; and

(c) If not, what steps are contemplated to remedy it?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT

(SHRI SYED SHAHNAWAZ HUSSAIN)

(a) & (b): Two statements, one language-wise and the other state-wise, of the publications received by the National Library, Calcutta under the Delivery of Books and Newspapers

(Public Libraries) Act 1954, are given at Annexures I and II. As reported by the National Library, they may not be receiving all the books published in the country.

(c) Concerted efforts have been made in the form of periodic campaigns to familiarise the publishers with their obligations under the law. Advertisements have been put out in the newspapers drawing attention to the provisions of the law. Advantage is also being taken of the major book fairs held periodically in different parts of the country. The National Library authorities have also been writing to major publishers from time to time about the need to fulfil their obligations under the law without exception. Such efforts are continuous process.

title: INVESTMENT BY IT INDUSTRY IN TECHNICAL EDUCATION .
question_text:
(a) Whether it is a fact that about Rs.2000 crores are required for technical education, to keep leadership in IT market;

(b) if so, whether Government would make the IT industry also share this investment;

(c) whether any blueprint has been prepared for this; and

(d) if so, the details thereof? ANSWER
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT

(DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI)


(a) to (d): The Task Force on HRD in IT in its Interim Report has made forty-seven (47) recommendations aimed at ensuring supply of adequate and quality IT manpower for both meeting the domestic and global demands. This shall be done by strengthening and upgrading the technical education system in the country. These recommendations relate to IT faculty development, IT curricula & courseware development, strengthening of computing and networking facilities, digitization and modernization of libraries, use of IT for administrative support services and promoting interface with industry. Investment required would be to the tune of Rs. 2000 crore (central share alone) over a seven year period (up to the end of 10th Five Year Plan). Most of the capital outlay would, however, be required in first 3-4 years only. These interventions would result in overall increase in availability of quality IT manpower in larger numbers in the country. This will help the Indian software services sector to increase its productivity and move up the value chain, enabling the country to capture larger share of global markets of IT software and services.

title: LIBRARY SCIENCE EDUCATION .
question_text:
(a) whether Government have any scheme for development of library science education in the country, in view of its being a developing country;

(b) whether any guideline has been issued to members of Lok Sabha/Rajya Sabha to allocate funds out of the M.P.L.A.D. Scheme for construction/operation of libraries in their respective Parliamentary Constituencies/States;

(c) if so, the details thereof;

(d) if not, whether Government propose to formulate any rules/directives in this regard; and

(e) if so, by when and if not, the reasons therefor?
answer_text:
THE MINISTER OF HUMAN RESOURCE DEVELOPMENT, SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY AND OCEAN DEVELOPMENT

(DR. MURLI MANOHAR JOSHI)

(a) According to the information furnished by University Grants Commission, the Commission provides development grant to Universities for development of Library Science Education.

(b) to (e): Under the Member of Parliament Local Area Development Scheme (MPLADS) of the Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation, works as recommended by the Members of Parliament, can be taken up for the creation of durable assets based on locally felt needs. That Ministry has issued detailed guidelines on the MPLADS. The illustrative list of works that can be taken up under the MPLADS includes construction of only buildings of public libraries and reading rooms. However, books, furniture and fixtures, etc., are not allowed to be purchased for the public libraries and reading rooms as there is specific restriction on purchase of inventory of stock of any type.

title: IMPLEMENTATION OF CENTRALLY SPONSORED SOCIAL WELFARE SCHEMES THROUGH NGOS .
question_text:
(a) the details of Centrally-sponsored social welfare schemes implemented through NGOs;

(b) the amount of grants sanctioned by Government in favour of each NGO working in Maharashtra during the last two years; and

(c) the measures taken or proposed to be taken for ensuring transparency in the working of NGOs to the target-oriented areas of development?
answer_text:
MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SOCIAL JUSTICE AND EMPOWERMENT (SMT. MANEKA GANDHI)
a) The details of Centrally-sponsored social welfare schemes implemented through NGOs by this Ministry is as under:- S.No Name of the Centrally-sponsored social welfare schemes implemented through NGOs 1 Promote Voluntary Action for Persons with Disabilities 2 Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase of Aids/Appliances 3 Prevention of Alcoholism & Substance (Drugs) Abuse 4 Grant-in-aid Programme for Assistance in the field of Social Defence 5 Integrated Programme for Street Children 6 Integrated Programme for Older Persons 7 Assistance to Panchayati Raj Instts./Vol.Orgns./self Help Groups for Construction of Old Age Homes/Multi Service Centers for Older Persons 8 Grant-in-aid to Voluntary Orgns. Working for the Welfare of Scheduled Castes 9 Scheme of Assistance to Vol. Orgns. Working for the Other Backward Classes 10 Schme of Pre-examination Coaching for the Other Backward Classes 11 Scheme of Pre-examination Coaching for Weaker Sections based on Economic Criteria.
b) The amount of grants sanctioned by the Government in favour of each NGO working in Maharashtra during the last two years is annexed. c) To ensure transparency in the working of these NGOs, the measures taken are
(i) periodical inspections are carried out through officers of this Ministry, Designated Agencies and the representatives of the State Government.
(ii)To elicit relevant physical and financial performance indicators, the Ministry has also issued fresh formats for applications for grants as well as for reporting of work done by these NGOs.
(iii)Details of the releases are made available in the Ministry`s website (socialjustice.nic.in). giving full publicity to releases made to NGOs. Members of Parliament, State Governments and District Magistrates have also been furnished district-wise lists of NGOs assisted by the Ministry in the last two years.
(iv) Besides the above, it is mandatory for all grantee organizations to submit utilization certificates before release of further installments to them.
